articles.title: Rochdale Appoints New Chief Executive
articles.excerpt: Rochdale has appointed Murphy as its new chief executive. The decision was made during a recent council meeting, where members expressed confidence in Murphy's ability to lead the organisation. Murphy brings a wealth of experience in local government and is expected to focus on improving services and engaging with the community.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale has appointed Murphy as its new chief executive. The decision was made during a recent council meeting, where members expressed confidence in Murphy's ability to lead the organisation.

Murphy brings a wealth of experience in local government and is expected to focus on improving services and engaging with the community.

The appointment follows a thorough selection process, with Murphy emerging as the preferred candidate due to their track record in similar roles.

Council officials are optimistic that Murphy's leadership will help drive forward key initiatives and address the challenges facing the borough.

articles.title: Reform UK pushes for borough-wide HMO controls across Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Reform UK councillors have called for stronger planning controls on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) across Rochdale Borough, arguing that residents should have a greater say over developments affecting their communities.
articles.summary: A motion brought before Rochdale Council by Reform UK councillors has reignited debate over the growth of HMOs, with the party calling for borough-wide planning restrictions while welcoming council recognition that the issue requires further consideration.
articles.content_html:
Reform UK councillors have renewed calls for stronger controls on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) across Rochdale Borough following a debate at a recent Full Council meeting.

The party brought forward a motion proposing a borough-wide Article 4 Direction, which would remove certain permitted development rights and require planning permission before new HMOs could be established.

According to Reform UK, the proposal was intended to give residents a greater opportunity to comment on developments that may affect their neighbourhoods before approval is granted.

Debate over planning powers

During the council debate, the original motion was amended before being considered by councillors.

Reform UK said it would have preferred immediate borough-wide protections but ultimately supported the amended version after it formally acknowledged concerns surrounding increasing HMO concentrations and committed the council to further work on the potential use of Article 4 powers.

The party argued that rejecting the amended motion entirely would have risked losing momentum on an issue that has generated significant discussion among residents in several parts of the borough.

Concerns over community impact

Supporters of the proposal say growing concentrations of HMOs can place additional pressure on local infrastructure, parking, public services and community cohesion.

Reform UK councillors argue that the current planning framework allows many HMOs to be created without the level of scrutiny that residents would expect for other forms of development.

The party believes local people should have a greater voice when significant changes are proposed within established residential communities.

Borough-wide approach proposed

One of the central arguments made during the debate was that restrictions should apply consistently across the borough rather than being introduced only in selected locations.

Reform UK says protecting one neighbourhood while leaving others unaffected could simply relocate development pressure elsewhere.

The party therefore continues to support a borough-wide Article 4 Direction covering Rochdale, Middleton, Heywood and the Pennines.

Issue remains on the political agenda

The councillors say the debate represents an important first step in securing wider recognition of residents' concerns.

While acknowledging that the outcome did not fully reflect their original proposal, Reform UK believes the discussion has ensured that HMO policy remains a live political issue within Rochdale Borough.

The council is expected to continue gathering evidence and reviewing potential planning options before any future decisions are made regarding Article 4 powers.

This article is based on statements published by Reform UK Rochdale Councillors following a Full Council debate regarding HMOs and planning policy. The views expressed are those of the party and form part of an ongoing political discussion concerning housing and planning within Rochdale Borough.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports statements made by elected councillors and political representatives regarding planning policy. The views described are political positions and do not represent adopted council policy unless formally approved by Rochdale Council.

Plans for the development of Bagslate Moor Road in Rochdale have been announced. The initiative aims to improve local infrastructure and services in the area.

articles.title: GM4Women2028 Event Unites Greater Manchester in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: The GM4Women2028 event took place in Rochdale, bringing together various stakeholders from across Greater Manchester to discuss women's issues and future initiatives.
articles.content_html:
A significant event called GM4Women2028 took place in Rochdale, aiming to unite stakeholders from across Greater Manchester. The gathering focused on addressing women's issues and promoting initiatives that support gender equality.

Participants included local leaders, community organisations, and advocates for women's rights, who engaged in discussions about future projects and collaborations.

The event highlighted the importance of collective action in tackling challenges faced by women in the region. It served as a platform for sharing ideas and fostering partnerships among attendees.

Organisers expressed optimism about the outcomes of the discussions, which are expected to lead to actionable plans aimed at improving the lives of women in Greater Manchester.

articles.title: Healey councillor welcomes first beehive to Number One Riverside roof
articles.excerpt: Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton has welcomed the installation of the first beehive on the roof of Number One Riverside in Rochdale town centre.
articles.summary: A new rooftop beehive supplied and maintained by Boro Bees will make use of the sedum roof and nearby riverbank wildflowers, with its first honey being left for the colony over winter.
articles.content_html:
Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton has welcomed the installation of the first beehive on the roof of Number One Riverside in Rochdale town centre.

The hive was installed with support from Boro Bees, which supplied the colony and will continue to maintain it.

Councillor Ayrton described the installation as exciting news and said the bees would benefit from the building’s sedum roof and the wildflowers growing along the nearby riverbank.

Supporting bees in the town centre

The rooftop location will give the bees access to pollen and nectar from plants around Number One Riverside, the River Roch and areas further afield.

Sedum roofs are planted with low-growing vegetation and can provide additional habitats and food sources for pollinating insects in built-up areas.

The project adds a new biodiversity feature to the council building while demonstrating how urban spaces can be adapted to support wildlife.

First honey to remain with the colony

Any honey produced during the colony’s first year will be left for the bees themselves.

This will help the colony establish itself and provide the food reserves needed to survive and remain healthy through the winter.

Those involved in the project hope the hive may produce surplus honey in future years once the colony is well established.

“The bees will benefit from the sedum roof on the top of Number One Riverside and from the wildflowers that grow along the riverbank.”

Hive to be maintained by local specialists

Boro Bees will be responsible for maintaining the hive and monitoring the health of the colony.

Photographs from the installation show a beekeeper placing frames containing the bees inside the wooden rooftop hive.

This report is based on a public update and photographs published by Healey ward councillor Tricia Ayrton following the installation of the beehive at Number One Riverside.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports a public update issued by an elected councillor. References to the environmental benefits and future honey production of the hive reflect information shared about the project.

articles.title: Green Party Candidate Geraldine Coggins Engages with Activists in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Geraldine Coggins, the Green Party candidate for Greater Manchester Mayor, spoke with local activists in Rochdale as part of her campaign efforts.
articles.content_html:
A Green Party candidate for Greater Manchester Mayor, Geraldine Coggins, engaged with activists in Rochdale on 16 July 2026. Coggins discussed her campaign and the party's policies during the meeting.

The event took place in Rochdale, where Coggins aims to connect with local communities and gather support ahead of the upcoming mayoral election.

Coggins's campaign focuses on environmental issues and community engagement, reflecting the Green Party's priorities.

articles.title: Rochdale Charity Raises Awareness of Honour-Based Abuse at Women’s Event
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Women's Welfare Association participated in the GM4Women Dialogue, focusing on honour-based abuse and women's equality at a Greater Manchester event.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 15 July 2026 at 22:40. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale Women's Welfare Association participated in the GM4Women Dialogue, focusing on honour-based abuse and women's equality at a Greater Manchester event.

Latest update

Rochdale Women's Welfare Association participated in the GM4Women Dialogue, focusing on honour-based abuse and women's equality at a Greater Manchester event.

A Rochdale charity has highlighted the issue of honour-based abuse at a recent women’s event in Greater Manchester. The event aimed to raise awareness and provide support for individuals affected by this form of abuse.

Representatives discussed the signs of honour-based abuse and the resources available for victims. They emphasised the importance of community support in addressing this issue.

Attendees were encouraged to engage in discussions about the impact of honour-based abuse. The charity aims to foster a safe environment for those affected to seek help.

Earlier developments

This initiative is part of a broader effort to combat honour-based abuse and promote understanding within the community.

Rochdale Women's Welfare Association participated in the annual GM4Women Dialogue, Deeds and Determination gathering, which brought together women and allies from across Greater Manchester to discuss equality and support for women and girls.

The event included workshops, panel discussions, and contributions from community leaders and elected representatives. The association's Independent Domestic Violence Adviser and Independent Sexual Violence Adviser, Rabiah, joined a plenary panel discussing honour-based violence affecting women and girls. Rabiah also led a workshop with the organisation's Strategic Lead, Rizwana, focusing on honour-based abuse, encouraging discussions on raising awareness and improving support for those affected.

Rochdale's Mayor, Councillor Faisal Rana, and the Mayoress, Mrs Iram Faisal, attended the event, where the Mayor emphasised the importance of supporting women and girls in local communities. Kate Green, Deputy Mayor of Greater Manchester for Safer and Stronger Communities, also attended, alongside Councillors Sameena and Amber Nisa.

Women from all ten Greater Manchester boroughs participated in the gathering, highlighting collaboration between organisations and communities addressing issues affecting women and girls. Rochdale Women's Welfare Association thanked GM4Women for organising the event, stating it provided an opportunity for women to have their voices heard and engage with decision makers.

Rochdale Women's Welfare Association highlighted honour-based abuse awareness during the annual GM4Women Dialogue, Deeds and Determination gathering held in Greater Manchester. The event brought together women and allies from across the region to discuss equality and support for women and girls.

The gathering featured workshops, panel discussions, and contributions from community leaders and elected representatives. The association's Independent Domestic Violence Adviser and Independent Sexual Violence Adviser, Rabiah, participated in a plenary panel discussing issues surrounding honour-based violence. Rabiah also led a workshop with the organisation's Strategic Lead, Rizwana, focusing on honour-based abuse, encouraging discussions on raising awareness and improving support for affected individuals.

The association noted that women from all ten Greater Manchester boroughs attended the event, showcasing collaboration among organisations and communities addressing issues affecting women and girls. Rochdale Women's Welfare Association expressed gratitude to GM4Women for organising the event, which provided a platform for women to share their experiences and engage with decision-makers.

Update timeline

  • 15 July 2026, 22:40: Rochdale Charity Raises Awareness of Honour-Based Abuse at Women’s Event
  • 15 July 2026, 22:39: Rochdale Charity Promotes Awareness of Honour-Based Abuse at Women’s Event

articles.title: Rochdale Council Budget Discussion Sparks Online Debate
articles.excerpt: A recent Reddit discussion about the Rochdale Council budget has drawn attention, highlighting community concerns and suggestions for local improvements.
articles.content_html:
A discussion on Reddit regarding the Rochdale Council budget has generated significant online engagement. Users have shared their thoughts on potential allocations and priorities for the upcoming fiscal year.

Participants in the thread have raised various issues, including funding for local services and infrastructure improvements. Suggestions have been made for enhancing community facilities within the borough.

The conversation reflects a growing interest in local governance and the impact of budget decisions on residents' daily lives. Many contributors expressed a desire for greater transparency in the budgeting process.

As the council prepares to finalise its budget, the online dialogue may influence future discussions regarding resource allocation in Rochdale.

articles.title: Adult Care Services in Rochdale Commended by CQC
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council's adult care services have received commendation from the Care Quality Commission for improvements in care quality.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has received positive feedback from the Care Quality Commission (CQC) regarding its adult care services. The CQC's latest report highlighted significant improvements in the quality of care provided to residents.

The council's adult care services were assessed on various criteria, including safety, effectiveness, and responsiveness. The CQC noted that the services have made considerable progress in meeting the needs of the community.

Councillor Shah Wazir expressed pride in the recognition, stating that the council is committed to enhancing the quality of care for all residents.

This commendation from the CQC is expected to bolster confidence in the adult care services offered in Rochdale.

articles.title: Petrus Receives Mayor of Rochdale Recognition Award
articles.excerpt: Petrus has been honoured with the Mayor of Rochdale Recognition Award for its commitment to supporting individuals facing homelessness and other challenges in the community.
articles.content_html:
A charity called Petrus has received the Mayor of Rochdale Recognition Award. The award acknowledges the charity's dedication to supporting people experiencing homelessness and other challenges in the community.

The recognition highlights the efforts of Petrus in providing essential services and support to vulnerable individuals. The charity's team has been commended for their hard work and commitment to improving lives.

In a statement, Petrus expressed gratitude for the award, thanking its staff for their dedication and service. The charity continues to strive towards making a positive impact in Rochdale.

The Mayor of Rochdale's Recognition Award is given to organisations and individuals who demonstrate exceptional service to the community, reflecting the values of compassion and support.

articles.title: Petition calls for defibrillators to be mandatory in all gyms
articles.excerpt: A parliamentary petition is calling for every gym and exercise establishment to be legally required to have a defibrillator available.
articles.summary: The petition, created by Eleanor Felton-Page, asks the Government to make automated external defibrillators mandatory in all gyms and exercise venues. It is due to close on 13 October 2026.
articles.content_html:
A parliamentary petition is calling for defibrillators to become a legal requirement in every gym and exercise establishment.

The petition was created by Eleanor Felton-Page and is open for signatures until 13 October 2026.

It asks the Government to require gyms and other exercise venues to make an automated external defibrillator, commonly known as an AED, readily available in case somebody suffers a cardiac arrest.

Call for quicker access to lifesaving equipment

The petition argues that rapid access to a defibrillator could improve a person's chances of survival when cardiac arrest happens during or shortly after exercise.

Its supporting statement claims that more than 90% of sudden cardiac arrests involving young athletes occur during or immediately after exercise.

It also states that using an AED within three to five minutes could increase survival rates significantly. These figures are claims presented by the petition organiser and have not been independently verified by Rochdale Daily.

What is a defibrillator?

An automated external defibrillator is a portable device designed to analyse a person's heart rhythm and, where appropriate, deliver an electric shock to help restore a normal rhythm.

The devices provide spoken or visual instructions and can be used by members of the public while emergency help is on its way.

Anyone who believes a person is in cardiac arrest should call 999 immediately, begin CPR when instructed and send somebody to locate the nearest defibrillator where possible.

How to sign the petition

People wishing to support the proposal can sign the petition on the UK Parliament website.

Under the parliamentary petitions system, petitions that reach 10,000 signatures normally receive a Government response, while those reaching 100,000 signatures are considered for debate in Parliament.

This report is based on the wording of UK Parliament petition 764204. Medical and statistical claims attributed to the petition reflect the organiser's published case for the proposal.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports the contents of a public petition and does not provide medical advice. Anyone who suspects a cardiac arrest should call 999 immediately and follow the emergency call handler's instructions.
articles.right_to_reply: The petition creator, the Department of Health and Social Care, gym operators and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Faisal Rana Becomes Rochdale's New Mayor
articles.excerpt: Faisal Rana, a former Navy officer born in Pakistan, has officially taken office as the new mayor of Rochdale, marking a significant milestone in the borough's leadership.
articles.content_html:
A former Navy officer born in Pakistan has assumed office as the new mayor of Rochdale. Faisal Rana took on the role on 12 July 2026, succeeding the previous mayor in a ceremony attended by local dignitaries.

Rana's appointment is notable as he becomes the first person of Pakistani descent to hold the mayoral position in Rochdale. His election reflects the borough's diverse community.

In his inaugural address, Rana expressed his dedication to serving all residents of Rochdale and addressing key issues facing the borough.

The new mayor's leadership is expected to focus on enhancing local services and fostering a sense of unity among residents.
